zh.lifeSummary: 當Hawise Payne or avis於1270年出生在英國戴文時，她的父親Thomas de Pyne27歲，母親Avis - Hawise De Shute22歲。她於大約1292年與Nicholas Bonville在英國英格蘭戴文書特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1295年在她的家鄉享年25歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hawise Payne or avis于1270年出生在英国戴文时，她的父亲Thomas de Pyne27岁，母亲Avis - Hawise De Shute22岁。她于大约1292年与Nicholas Bonville在英国英格兰戴文書特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1295年在她的家乡享年25岁。

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: avis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Middle English and Old French female personal name Avice, which may be from ancient Germanic Aveza, but more probably from Latin Avitia (also found in a masculine form, Avitius). In France, this surname is rare.
Portuguese and Spanish: habitational name from the little town of Avis (the stress is on the i) in Portalegre district (Portugal). This surname is found in the neighboring Spanish Extremadura.
Dutch: variant of Aafjes a metronymic from the female personal name Ave, which is of unclear origin.

variants.name: Avins
meanings: English: metronymic from the Middle English female personal name Avine, which is probably a pet form of Avis .

variants.name: Havis
meanings: English: variant of Avis with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Avison
meanings: English (Yorkshire): metronymic from the Middle English female personal name Avice (see Avis ).

variants.name: Havice
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of English   Avis  .

variants.name: Avon
meanings: English:

habitational name from Avon (Wiltshire) or Little or Middle Avon (Gloucestershire), both named from the river on which they stand, or possibly a habitational name from a settlement on one of the rivers or small streams called Avon or Aven. These river names derive from the Celtic word for ‘river’, as reflected in Welsh afon and Gaelic abhainn. The modern surname is concentrated in Somerset and Wiltshire, England, suggesting it is associated chiefly with the Avon river that rises on the Gloucester-Wiltshire border and flows through Wiltshire and Somerset into the Severn.
from the Middle English female personal name Avine, which is probably a pet form of Avis .

nameMeanings.name: Hawise
meanings: This female name (which has been corrupted down to Avice and Avis) is from Hadewisa, formed from the German name Hedvig, same as Edwig, which Wachter translates 'propugnator felicitatis;' say, 'fortunate soldier.'

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Hawise Payne or avis was born in 1270, in Devon, England, her father, Thomas de Pyne, was 27 and her mother, Avis - Hawise De Shute, was 22. She married Nicholas Bonville about 1292, in Shute, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1295, in her hometown, at the age of 25.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hawise Payne or avis est née en 1270, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Pyne, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Avis - Hawise De Shute, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas Bonville environ 1292, à Shute, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1295, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 25 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hawise Payne or avis 1270 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas de Pyne 27 und ihre Mutter Avis - Hawise De Shute 22. Sie hat um 1292 in Shute, Devon, England, United Kingdom Nicholas Bonville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1295 im Alter von 25 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hawise Payne or avis è nata il nel 1270 nel Devon, England, sua padre, Thomas de Pyne, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Avis - Hawise De Shute, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Nicholas Bonville circa 1292, nel Shute, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1295, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 25 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Hawise Payne or avis(이)가 1270년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Pyne(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Avis - Hawise De Shute(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1292년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 슈트에서 Nicholas Bonville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1295년 그녀의 고향에서 25세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hawise Payne or avis nasceu em 1270, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Pyne, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Avis - Hawise De Shute, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Nicholas Bonville aproximadamente 1292, em Shute, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1295, em sua cidade natal, com 25 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hawise Payne or avis родилась в 1270 г. в местности девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas de Pyne было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Avis - Hawise De Shute, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas Bonville около 1292 г. в местности шут, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1295 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 25 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hawise Payne or avis nació en 1270, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de Pyne, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Avis - Hawise De Shute, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Nicholas Bonville aproximadamente 1292, en Shute, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1295, en su pueblo, a la edad de 25 años.
